title: Gherkin/BDD prompt language experiment for recipe step executor generation

generation_target.summary: Generate a recipe step executor in Python with conditional execution, step dependencies (DAG), retry with exponential backoff, timeout handling, output capture with template resolution, and sub-recipe delegation with context isolation. The key complexity lies in cross-feature interactions: retried step outputs feeding conditions, timeout-blocked conditional steps, skip-vs-fail propagation, and sub-recipe failure triggering parent retry.
deliverables: RecipeStepExecutor class that accepts a recipe (list of step dicts) and a context dict.
Conditional execution: evaluate condition expressions against context, skip when false or key missing.
Step dependencies: blockedBy relationships forming a DAG. Failed deps propagate failure. Skipped deps do not.
Retry with exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s delays. Timed-out steps are NOT retried.
Timeout handling: terminate step at timeout_seconds, mark as timed_out, propagate as failure to dependents.
Output capture: store step output in context under step ID. Template syntax {{step_id}} for interpolation.
Sub-recipe delegation: child context inherits parent, outputs isolated unless propagate_outputs is true. Failed sub-recipe never propagates.
Focused tests covering each feature independently AND cross-feature interactions.
non_goals: Do not implement a CLI or REST API around the executor.
Do not implement persistent storage of execution state.
Do not implement distributed execution across machines.
Do not implement a recipe definition DSL or parser beyond dict/JSON input.

prompt_variants.label: Hybrid Gherkin plus English guidance prompt
Gherkin plus explicit acceptance criteria prompt

fairness_rules: All variants target the same recipe step executor generation task.
The hybrid prompt may add implementation guidance but must not expand the required deliverables.
The acceptance criteria variant may add quality requirements but must not widen scope beyond the same executor.
Prompt reviewers should compare deliverables and non-goals before any full-matrix run.
ownership_notes: This repo owns the experiment assets, manifest, and scoring utilities.
V2 redesign: replaced user-auth-API ceiling task with harder recipe-step-executor target.
V1 results preserved in git history (user_auth_api target, PR #3964 initial commit).
